title: Reduce inaccurate rest_fallback usage and preserve live stream mark authority

context.operator_report: Fallback pricing is inaccurate and appears more often than true live market prices on the dashboard.
trace_summary: dashboard_server.py _stream_positions_mark() maps mark.get('source') into positions payload field 'mark_source'
dashboard.html intentionally renders rest_fallback as 'mark REST' and poll_fallback as 'mark POLL'
Current trace classified mark REST as expected fallback behavior, but fallback appears active often enough to create operator trust issues
context.problem_statement: The issue is no longer whether the UI chip is correct. The issue is that fallback marks appear to be too frequent and sometimes inaccurate, making live card pricing feel untrustworthy.

objective: Implement the smallest safe fix so live stream marks remain the primary authority whenever they are fresh, and rest_fallback only takes over when the live stream mark is truly missing or stale beyond the intended threshold.
conditional_allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\state_hub.py (ONLY if needed to preserve or compare mark timestamps/source authority correctly)
forbidden_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\polymarket_stream.py unless manager approval is explicitly required by blocking evidence
forbidden_actions: Read every file in the repo
Change dashboard wording unrelated to mark-source reliability
required_work: Inspect the exact interaction between _poll_stale_mark(...) and _stream_positions_mark() in dashboard_server.py
Identify how a fresh stream mark can be overwritten, superseded, or outvoted by a fallback mark
Patch the logic so stream marks remain authoritative when fresh
Ensure rest_fallback engages only when stream data is actually missing or stale beyond the intended threshold
Ensure the chosen mark_source accurately reflects the real source used for the final displayed mark
Add minimal, non-spammy logging sufficient to verify why fallback was chosen when it happens
acceptance_criteria: Fresh stream marks are not replaced by older or lower-authority fallback marks
rest_fallback is used only when stream marks are missing or stale beyond threshold
mark_source accurately reflects the source of the displayed mark
Dashboard cards show stream-driven prices whenever the live stream is healthy for that market
No files outside allowed scope are modified
python -m py_compile core/state_hub.py passes if state_hub.py is modified
verification_required: Show the exact code path changed
Explain the old overwrite/fallback behavior and the new behavior
Confirm whether state_hub.py was touched or not
Run py_compile on every changed Python file
State whether dashboard process restart is required for the fix to take effect
